definitions.costal part of respiratory diaphragm mus: A part of the respiratory diaphragm muscle that arises from the inner surfaces of the lower six costal cartilages and the lower four ribs, forming a thin domed sheet of radially arranged myofibers extending laterally from the ribs and converging medially onto the central tendon; constitutes the primary muscular domain driving thoracic cavity expansion during inspiration.

costal part of respiratory diaphragm mus.note: UBERON:0035831 already covers this concept. The NTR label 'costal part of respiratory diaphragm muscle' can be added as an additional synonym. No new UBERON ID should be minted.

parent_correction.rationale: UBERON:0000975 is 'sternum', which is anatomically incorrect for a part of the respiratory diaphragm muscle. The correct parent is UBERON:0001103 (diaphragm / thoracic diaphragm).
